summary.L1_headline: SEM – Announces $16.50 per share merger agreement. | 03/02/26
summary.L2_brief: Select Medical Holdings Corporation entered into a definitive merger agreement with Stallion Intermediate Corporation (backed by WCAS XIV) to be acquired for $16.50 per share in cash. The deal requires both majority stockholder approval and a separate majority-of-the-minority vote, with an outside date of December 1, 2026 (extendable to March 1, 2027). Company termination fee is $66.5M and reverse termination fee is $133M, with committed equity and debt financing in place.

L3_detailed.event: Select Medical entered into Agreement and Plan of Merger with Stallion Intermediate Corporation (Parent) and Stallion MergerSub Corporation to be taken private
key_figures: Equity financing from WCAS XIV, L.P.
L3_detailed.deal_implications: Transaction has board and special committee unanimous approval. No financing condition exists as committed equity and debt financing secured. Deal structure includes rollover shares for certain holders, indicating management/insider participation in go-private transaction.
remaining_conditions: Majority approval of outstanding shares entitled to vote
Majority-of-the-minority vote (excluding Parent, Merger Sub, Rollover Holders and affiliates)
HSR Act waiting period expiration or termination
No Material Adverse Effect on the Company
risks_flagged: Dual voting requirement including majority-of-the-minority creates additional approval hurdle
Healthcare regulatory approvals required beyond standard HSR clearance
Forward-looking statements warn of potential litigation related to the proposed merger
Rollover Holders deemed participants in solicitation - potential conflicts of interest

summary.filer_name: Robert A. Ortenzio, Martin F. Jackson, Estate of Rocco A. Ortenzio
summary.L1_headline: + SEM – Robert A. Ortenzio (group) discloses 11.3% stake with $16.00/share take-private proposal. | 02/23/26
summary.L2_brief: Robert A. Ortenzio (Executive Chairman and co-founder), Martin F. Jackson (Senior EVP), and the Estate of Rocco A. Ortenzio filed an amended 13D disclosing a combined beneficial ownership of approximately 15.7% of Select Medical Holdings. The amendment reports that on February 22, 2026, Ortenzio delivered a refined non-binding proposal to the Special Committee to acquire all outstanding shares not already owned by the group at $16.00 per share in cash. This is a management-led take-private attempt by company insiders.

ownership_details.shares_held: Robert A. Ortenzio: 14,019,735; Martin F. Jackson: 1,383,421; Estate of Rocco A. Ortenzio: 4,085,550 (with significant overlap)
ownership_details.percentage_owned: Robert A. Ortenzio: 11.3%; Martin F. Jackson: 1.1%; Estate of Rocco A. Ortenzio: 3.3%
ownership_details.sole_voting_power: Robert A. Ortenzio: 7,081,788; Martin F. Jackson: 1,383,421; Estate: 0
ownership_details.shared_voting_power: Robert A. Ortenzio: 6,937,947; Martin F. Jackson: 2,634; Estate: 4,085,550
ownership_details.sole_dispositive_power: Robert A. Ortenzio: 7,081,788; Martin F. Jackson: 1,383,421; Estate: 0
ownership_details.shared_dispositive_power: Robert A. Ortenzio: 6,937,947; Martin F. Jackson: 2,634; Estate: 4,085,550

L3_detailed.position_change: Amendment to original Schedule 13D filed November 24, 2025; no transactions in Issuer stock within past 60 days - filing triggered by refined buyout proposal, not position change
L3_detailed.source_of_funds: OO (Other) - Shares acquired through compensation for roles as officers/directors and ownership prior to IPO as co-founders
L3_detailed.purpose_of_transaction: On February 22, 2026, Mr. Ortenzio delivered a refined non-binding proposal to the Special Committee of the Board to acquire all outstanding Shares not already owned by the Reporting Persons at a price of $16.00 per share, payable in cash. This is a management buyout (MBO) proposal to take the company private.
L3_detailed.activist_intentions: Active take-private proposal. The filers are seeking to acquire 100% of shares they do not already own. Robert A. Ortenzio is Executive Chairman and co-founder; Martin F. Jackson is Senior EVP of Strategic Finance and Operations. This is an insider-led buyout attempt with full intent to gain control and delist the company.
L3_detailed.deal_implications: This is a potential take-private transaction led by management insiders controlling approximately 11-16% of shares (depending on overlap calculation). The proposal is subject to Special Committee approval and regulatory approvals. The filing explicitly warns that discussions may be terminated at any time without prior notice and there is no assurance a definitive agreement will be reached. Key risks include Special Committee rejection, financing contingencies, and regulatory hurdles. As an insider-led deal, there may be heightened scrutiny of fairness and potential for competing bids from third parties.
L3_detailed.related_agreements: None explicitly mentioned. The refined proposal letter (Exhibit 99.3) is referenced but not detailed in the filing. No standstill or voting agreements disclosed.
risks_flagged: Management buyout with potential conflicts of interest requiring Special Committee scrutiny
No definitive agreement - proposal is non-binding and may be terminated without notice
Financing contingencies not detailed in filing
Regulatory approval required
Potential for competing bids given insider proposal
Special Committee may reject proposal or negotiate higher price

summary.L1_headline: + SEM – Annual meeting proxy with board declassification vote. | 02/20/26
summary.L2_brief: Select Medical Holdings filed a preliminary proxy statement for its April 23, 2026 annual meeting. Key proposals include electing three Class II directors, a vote to declassify the board structure, and competing proposals on special meeting thresholds (Company proposes 25%, stockholder proposes 10%). No M&A transaction is pending; this is routine annual governance.

L3_detailed.filing_purpose: Preliminary proxy statement for the 2026 Annual Meeting of Stockholders to be held April 23, 2026, soliciting votes on director elections, executive compensation, auditor ratification, board declassification, and special meeting rights.
key_information: 124,018,300 shares outstanding as of record date February 27, 2026 with 134 registered holders
Board recommends FOR declassification proposal to phase out classified board structure
Company proposes 25% ownership threshold for stockholders to call special meetings; stockholder counter-proposal seeks 10% threshold
8 of 10 directors determined to be independent; Russell L. Carson serves as Lead Director
Board diversity: 2 female directors, 8 male directors; 1 Asian director identified
financial_highlights: NEO stock ownership requirements: 3.0x base salary for Executive Chairman, Vice Chairman, and CEO; 1.5x for other NEOs
2025 say-on-pay vote results: 108,011,565 votes for, 11,314,550 against (approximately 90.5% approval)
L3_detailed.deal_relevance: No M&A transaction disclosed or pending. Filing is routine annual proxy solicitation with governance proposals. Board declassification could make the company marginally more susceptible to hostile takeover attempts if approved.
L3_detailed.regulatory_mentions: SEC, NYSE listing standards for director independence and audit committee requirements, Sarbanes-Oxley Act compliance, NIST Cybersecurity Framework, HIPAA Security Risk Assessment
timeline_or_dates: March 4, 2026 - Intended mailing date for Notice of Internet Availability
April 10, 2026 - Stockholder list available for examination
April 23, 2026 - Annual Meeting date at 11:00 a.m. EDT
conditions_or_requirements: Declassification proposal requires affirmative vote of majority of outstanding shares
Director elections require majority of votes cast (votes FOR must exceed votes AGAINST)
Proposals 2, 3, 5, and 6 require affirmative vote of majority of shares present or represented by proxy
risks_flagged: Cybersecurity and information security risks acknowledged; company has not experienced material breach in past three fiscal years
Company discloses risks including regulatory risk, credit risk, liquidity risk, reputational risk, and interest rate fluctuation risk
Third-party information security breaches have occurred but none with material impact

summary.L1_headline: + SEM – Strong rehab/hospital ops; take-private proposal pending. | 02/01/26
summary.L2_brief: Select Medical Holdings filed its FY2025 10-K reporting $5.45B revenue across 104 critical illness recovery hospitals, 38 rehabilitation hospitals, and 1,917 outpatient clinics. The company is subject to a pending take-private proposal from Executive Chairman Robert Ortenzio at $16.00-$16.20 per share, with an independent Special Committee evaluating the offer and strategic alternatives.

L3_detailed.filing_purpose: Annual report for fiscal year ended December 31, 2025, providing comprehensive financial and operational disclosure following the November 2024 spin-off of Concentra Group Holdings.
key_information: Operates 104 critical illness recovery hospitals (70 HIH, 34 free-standing), 38 rehabilitation hospitals, and 1,917 outpatient rehab clinics across 39 states and DC
Robert Ortenzio submitted non-binding take-private proposal at $16.00-$16.20 per share on November 24, 2025
Special Committee formed November 25, 2025 to evaluate proposal and strategic alternatives
Completed tax-free distribution of Concentra (104.1M shares) on November 25, 2024
Approximately 45,300 total employees at December 31, 2025
financial_highlights: Total revenue of $5,452.8 million for FY2025
Revenue mix: ~45% critical illness recovery hospitals, ~24% rehabilitation hospitals, ~24% outpatient rehabilitation
Medicare represents 28.6% of revenue; commercial insurance 51.6%
L3_detailed.deal_relevance: Active take-private proposal at $16.00-$16.20 per share from Executive Chairman Ortenzio. Special Committee is evaluating the proposal and other strategic alternatives. Aggregate market value of non-affiliate shares was approximately $1.56B at June 30, 2025. The outcome of this evaluation could result in acquisition of the company or alternative strategic transaction.
L3_detailed.regulatory_mentions: Medicare certification requirements for LTCHs and IRFs; Joint Commission, DNV, and CIHQ accreditation; CMS quality reporting requirements; state Medicaid programs participation; potential regulatory changes to Medicare/Medicaid reimbursement noted as ongoing risk factor
timeline_or_dates: November 24, 2025: Take-private proposal received at $16.00-$16.20 per share
conditions_or_requirements: Special Committee evaluation ongoing with advisors
Medicare certification maintenance required for critical illness and rehabilitation hospitals
Hospital-within-hospital arrangements must maintain separate qualification from host hospitals
risks_flagged: Changes in government reimbursement policies could reduce revenue and profitability
Shortages in qualified nurses, therapists, and physicians could limit staffing capacity
Increased dependence on contract labor driving up operating costs
Potential impact of take-private evaluation process on business operations
Competition may limit growth and reduce profitability
Cybersecurity risks and HIPAA compliance requirements

summary.filer_name: Robert A. Ortenzio, Martin F. Jackson, and Estate of Rocco A. Ortenzio
summary.L1_headline: + SEM – Ortenzio family/insiders disclose 15.7% stake with $16.00-$16.20 go-private proposal. | 11/24/25
summary.L2_brief: Executive Chairman Robert A. Ortenzio (11.3%), SVP Martin F. Jackson (1.1%), and the Estate of Rocco A. Ortenzio (3.3%) filed a Schedule 13D converting from 13G following submission of a non-binding go-private proposal to acquire all outstanding shares not already owned by the group at $16.00-$16.20 per share in cash. The filing group, consisting of company founders and senior executives, collectively controls approximately 15.7% of Select Medical and is seeking to take the company private, subject to Board approval, customary due diligence, and regulatory approvals.

ownership_details.shares_held: Robert A. Ortenzio: 14,019,735; Martin F. Jackson: 1,383,421; Estate of Rocco A. Ortenzio: 4,085,550 (significant overlap due to shared holdings)
ownership_details.percentage_owned: Robert A. Ortenzio: 11.3%; Martin F. Jackson: 1.1%; Estate of Rocco A. Ortenzio: 3.3%
ownership_details.sole_voting_power: Robert A. Ortenzio: 7,081,788; Martin F. Jackson: 1,383,421; Estate: 0
ownership_details.shared_voting_power: Robert A. Ortenzio: 6,937,947; Martin F. Jackson: 2,634; Estate: 4,085,550
ownership_details.sole_dispositive_power: Robert A. Ortenzio: 7,081,788; Martin F. Jackson: 1,383,421; Estate: 0
ownership_details.shared_dispositive_power: Robert A. Ortenzio: 6,937,947; Martin F. Jackson: 2,634; Estate: 4,085,550

L3_detailed.position_change: Conversion from Schedule 13G to 13D due to submission of go-private proposal; no transactions in the past 60 days reported
L3_detailed.source_of_funds: OO (Other) - Shares acquired as compensation for officer/director roles and shares owned prior to IPO as co-founders
L3_detailed.purpose_of_transaction: On November 24, 2025, the Reporting Persons submitted a non-binding proposal to acquire all outstanding shares not already owned by them at $16.00-$16.20 per share in cash. The proposal is subject to customary due diligence, Board approval, and regulatory approvals. Filing states: 'Mr. Ortenzio delivered a non-binding proposal (the "Proposal Letter") to the Board to offer to acquire all such Shares at a price of $16.00 to $16.20 per share, payable in cash.'
L3_detailed.activist_intentions: Active go-private proposal submitted. As Executive Chairman and senior executives, the filers 'regularly engage in discussions with management of the Issuer, the Board, other shareholders of the Issuer and other relevant parties, which discussions may include matters ranging from the operations and conduct of the Issuer's business to considering or exploring extraordinary corporate transactions.' If the transaction is not consummated, filers reserve the right to engage in any actions under Item 4(a)-(j) of Schedule 13D including acquiring additional shares or selling existing holdings.
L3_detailed.deal_implications: This is a management-led buyout (MBO) proposal. The insider group controlling ~15.7% of shares is seeking to take the company private. Key considerations: (1) Board must form special committee to evaluate; (2) Transaction requires approval from unaffiliated shareholders; (3) Offer price of $16.00-$16.20 will be scrutinized for fairness; (4) Financing sources referenced but not detailed ('certain other financing sources'); (5) No assurance given that definitive agreement will be reached or transaction consummated.
L3_detailed.related_agreements: Joint Filing Agreement dated November 24, 2025 among the Reporting Persons (Exhibit 99.1); Proposal Letter dated November 24, 2025 (Exhibit 99.2). Robert A. Ortenzio has shared voting/dispositive power over multiple family trusts and foundations including Rocco A. Ortenzio Revocable Trust (4,028,767 shares) and various family trusts.
risks_flagged: Management buyout creates inherent conflicts of interest requiring special committee review
Deal uncertainty - filing explicitly states 'no assurance' discussions will result in definitive agreement
Subject to completion of customary due diligence by the buyer group
Regulatory approval risk
Financing not fully detailed - references 'certain other financing sources'
If deal fails, insiders may acquire additional shares or sell existing holdings
Potential for competing bids given public disclosure of go-private interest

summary.L1_headline: + SEM – Q3 revenue up 7.2% YoY; Concentra fully separated. | 10/30/25
summary.L2_brief: Select Medical Holdings filed its Q3 2025 10-Q reporting quarterly revenue of $1.36B (up 7.2% YoY) and net income of $44.2M from continuing operations. The company completed the spin-off of Concentra in Q4 2024, and now operates three segments: critical illness recovery hospitals, rehabilitation hospitals, and outpatient rehabilitation. No active M&A transactions disclosed, though the company maintains equity method investments including a recent hospital contribution to Banner JV.

Medicare represents significant portion of revenue - subject to CMS reimbursement rate changes

summary.L1_headline: SEM – Board adopts amended bylaws for proxy rules. | 08/04/25
summary.L2_brief: Select Medical Holdings' Board approved amended and restated bylaws on July 30, 2025, enhancing stockholder nomination procedures in response to SEC universal proxy rules. The changes require stockholders to solicit proxies from holders of at least 67% of voting power for director nominations. This is a governance update with no direct merger deal implications.
